sections.title: Executive Summary
sections.promptHint: 3–5 sentences. Describe the competitive landscape, the top strategic finding, and preview the recommendation. Write this after the rest of the document to ensure accuracy.
sections.ragQuery: competitive landscape market summary key findings

sections.title: Market Overview
sections.promptHint: Define the market being analyzed: who the buyers are, what they are buying, market size and growth rate if known, and the 2–3 key trends currently driving competition.
sections.ragQuery: market definition size growth trends buyers dynamics

sections.title: Competitor Profiles
sections.promptHint: 2–5 competitor profiles, each covering: company overview, target customer segment, core value proposition, key features or capabilities, pricing model (if known), and 2–3 honest strengths and weaknesses.
sections.ragQuery: competitors company profiles features positioning strengths weaknesses

sections.title: Feature Comparison Matrix
sections.promptHint: A table with competitors as columns and key capabilities as rows. Use ✓, ✗, or a rating (1–3) for each cell. Choose 8–15 dimensions that differentiate competitors. Include the reader's own company if applicable.

sections.title: Pricing Comparison
sections.promptHint: Compare pricing models and ranges across competitors. Normalize to the same unit where possible (e.g. per seat per month, per API call). Note if pricing is not publicly available.
sections.ragQuery: pricing plans cost tiers subscription enterprise

sections.title: Strategic Positioning Map
sections.promptHint: Describe the competitive landscape using two key axes (e.g. Price vs. Feature depth; SMB vs. Enterprise; Build vs. Buy). Place each competitor on this 2x2 and explain what each quadrant means strategically.

sections.title: SWOT Analysis
sections.promptHint: SWOT for the reader's own company or product: Strengths (internal advantages vs. competitors), Weaknesses (internal gaps), Opportunities (market trends to exploit), Threats (competitor moves or market shifts). Use a 2x2 table format.
sections.ragQuery: strengths weaknesses opportunities threats competitive position

sections.title: Strategic Recommendations
sections.promptHint: 3–5 concrete actions the reader should take, each with a rationale grounded in the analysis. Each recommendation should be specific enough to act on. Prioritize by impact.
